# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:3.9-slim AS web

# Install web server dependencies
RUN pip install flask

# Copy web server code
COPY ./web /app/web
WORKDIR /app/web

# Expose web server port
EXPOSE 80

# Start web server
CMD ["python", "app.py"]

FROM node:14 AS api

# Install API server dependencies
WORKDIR /app/api
COPY ./api/package.json ./api/package-lock.json ./
RUN npm install

# Copy API server code
COPY ./api /app/api

# Expose API server port
EXPOSE 8080

# Start API server
CMD ["npm", "start"]

FROM postgres:13 AS db

# Set environment variables for database
ENV POSTGRES_USER=secure_user
ENV POSTGRES_PASSWORD=secure_password
ENV POSTGRES_DB=secure_db

# Expose database port
EXPOSE 5432

# Start database
CMD ["postgres"]

FROM redis:6 AS redis

# Expose Redis port
EXPOSE 6379

# Start Redis
CMD ["redis-server"]

# Final multi-service container
FROM nginx:alpine

# Copy web and API server builds
COPY --from=web /app/web /usr/share/nginx/html/web
COPY --from=api /app/api /usr/share/nginx/html/api

# Copy Nginx configuration
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf

# Expose all necessary ports
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

# Start Nginx
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
